sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.46 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A group action on a compact space is said to be if the set of points with trivial stabilizer is dense. Let$G$be a locally compact group, and$_{sp} G$its Furstenberg boundary (the largest minimal and strongly proximal compact$G$-space). Let$_1$and$_2$be two lattices in$G$, both acting faithfully on$_{sp} G$. [r]{a)} Is it possible that the$_1$-action on$_{sp} G$is topologically free, but the$_2$-ac...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
